laws.title: Public Law 19-144 | Public Area Management Act (PAMA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. This is a free-standing statute regulating road and sidewalk closures in the District of Columbia by various agencies, and section 9 repeals specified subsections of Public Law 15-95. No self-repeal or sunset appears in the source.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Short-title, severability, and effective-date note only.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Public Area Management Act' or 'PAMA'."

provisions.text_summary: Purpose and definitions for district closures, roads, sidewalks, armed forces, and the National Guard.
provisions.exact_change: Purpose and definitions note only.
provisions.evidence: "The purpose of this act is to prevent abuse by law enforcement in closing roads and sidewalks..."

provisions.text_summary: Closure authority for armed forces and specified agencies; bar on closing roads around the gun store.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the closure-authority rules as actual text, not a note.
provisions.evidence: "When deployed in the district by the President of the United States, the armed forces shall have the authority to close any public roads..."
provisions.notes: References PL 17-136 (Parks Act) and PL 17-126 (Special Events Act) as in force.

provisions.text_summary: Permitted reasons for closures, 24-hour limit, and citizen re-entry rules.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the closure-limit rules as actual text, not a note.
provisions.evidence: "Any road closure must be made in the interest of public health, safety, national security..."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 15-95 section VI(2), (3), and (4) in their entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal-mark PL 15-95 §VI(2), (3), and (4).
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 15-95, Section VI(2)(3) and (4) shall be repealed in their entirety."

current_implementation.detail: Draft inserted only a boilerplate note at the Title 54 anchor and did not make the repeal-marking or permanent textual edits required by the source.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to PL 15-95 §VI(2)-(4).
Codify the closure-authority and limitation rules as actual text rather than a note.
Remove the Trello link.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.

laws.title: Public Law 19-148 | Badges and Entrapment Act (BEA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The Act requires badge display and creates an entrapment defense, while only using 18 U.S.C. 1515(a)(4) as a definitional cross-reference and amending no numbered Code section directly.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Short-title and general note only.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Badges and Entrapment Act' or 'BEA'."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for badge, entrapment, officer, and government official.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the definitional framework as new Title 18 text rather than a note.
provisions.evidence: "The term 'officer' means any member of a law enforcement, or, persons defined in 18 U.S. Code 1515(a)(4)."

provisions.text_summary: Badge-display requirement for officers, subject to safety and undercover exceptions.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the badge-display rule as new Title 18 text.
provisions.evidence: "All officers must display a badge when in performance of their official duties..."

provisions.text_summary: Entrapment defense and penalties for officers who entrap or proceed knowingly.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the entrapment-defense framework as new Title 18 text.
provisions.evidence: "Entrapment shall be an official defence in criminal trials. It shall have two elements to it;"
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 18 section.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t50/s1 and did not codify the substantive criminal-procedure rules into Title 18.

recommended_actions: Codify sections 4-6 as new Title 18 sections rather than a Title 50 note.
Remove the Trello link.
Correct the placement to Title 18.

laws.title: Public Law 19-149 | Criminal Justice Reform Act (CJRA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 5 rewrites 18 U.S.C. 3156(a)(3), section 6 repeals Public Law 2-5, and section 7 resets maximum sentences for numerous Title 18 offenses and chapters. Nothing in the source self-repeals the Act itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and general provision.
provisions.exact_change: Short-title and general note only.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Criminal Justice Reform Act' or 'CJRA'."

provisions.text_summary: 18 U.S.C. 3156(a)(3) rewritten so felony means an offense punishable by more than eight days imprisonment.
provisions.exact_change: Replace subsection (a)(3) with the enacted felony-definition text.
provisions.evidence: "18 U.S. Code 3156(a)(3) shall now read as-"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 2-5 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal-mark PL 2-5 wherever carried.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 2-5 shall be repealed in its entirety."

provisions.text_summary: Resets maximum imprisonment for many Title 18 provisions and chapters.
provisions.exact_change: Apply the specified day-limit changes to each enumerated section and chapter-wide limit.
provisions.evidence: "The maximum imprisonment time for U.S. Code 1111, Murder, shall not exceed nine days."
provisions.notes: The chapter-wide and catch-all limits need note-level handling where the source is non-section-specific.

provisions.text_summary: Forty-day accumulated-sentence cap and judicial enforcement rule.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the sentence-cap framework as new Title 18 text.
provisions.evidence: "No Judge may issue a Judgement that constitutes a sentence longer than forty days..."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t18/s1 and did not apply the section 5 rewrite, section 6 repeal-marking, or the section 7 penalty changes.

recommended_actions: Apply amend-existing-text to 18 U.S.C. 3156(a)(3) and the section 7 penalty targets.
Apply repeal-marking to Public Law 2-5.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-150 | Small Businesses Empowerment Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. The Act is a free-standing administrative statute directing the Department of Commerce and Labor to establish small-business recognition, employment, and support programs, and it does not self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, which the body text gives as 'Small Business Empowerment and Support Act.'
provisions.exact_change: Short-title note only.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Small Business Empowerment and Support Act.'"
provisions.notes: The manifest title differs from the body short title.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose and definitions for small-business recognition, employment initiatives, and support services.
provisions.exact_change: Purpose and definitions note only.
provisions.evidence: "Small businesses are crucial for community economic prosperity..."

provisions.text_summary: Department of Commerce and Labor must establish a Small Business Recognition Program.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the programmatic directive as Title 15 text or a concise statutory note.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall establish a Small Business Recognition Program..."

provisions.text_summary: Department of Commerce and Labor must initiate a Small Business Employment Initiative.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the employment initiative as Title 15 text or a concise statutory note.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall initiate a Small Business Employment Initiative..."

provisions.text_summary: Department of Commerce and Labor must establish a Small Business Support Center.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the support-center directive as Title 15 text or a concise statutory note.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall establish a Small Business Support Center within the Department of Commerce and Labor."

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective-date clause.
provisions.exact_change: Effective-date note only.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall take effect immediately upon passage through both houses of Congress and upon the signature of the President."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t15/s1 and did not implement the programmatic Department of Commerce and Labor directives as text.

recommended_actions: Codify the small-business programs as Title 15 text or a concise statutory note.
Reconcile the short-title discrepancy between the body text and the manifest title in editorial notes.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-151 | Court Fees Prohibition and Protection Act (CTPPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 4 repeals a set of Title 28 fee sections in whole and section 5 imposes a free-standing prohibition on court fees. No self-repeal or sunset appears in the source.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Short-title and general note only.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Court Fees Prohibition and Protection Act' or 'CTPPA'"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the enumerated 28 U.S.C. fee and court-deposit sections in whole, including subsection 1918(b) only.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal-mark each listed section, with 1918 limited to subsection (b) only and 1932 de-duplicated.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S. Code 1911 shall be repealed in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Reconcile overlapping repeals already reserved by PL 17-133.

provisions.text_summary: No court may impose or collect fees except as punishment for a criminal offense or contempt of court; Congress sets any fees.
provisions.exact_change: Codify the fee-prohibition rule as a new Title 28 section.
provisions.evidence: "No Court in the United States, including the Supreme Court, shall impose, collect, or otherwise require fees in any of its proceedings unless imposed as a punishment for a criminal offence or contempt of court."
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 28 section.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t28/s1 and did not make the required repeal-markings or codify the section 5 prohibition.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to each section listed in SEC 4, de-duplicating 1932 and reconciling the PL 17-133 overlap.
Codify SEC 5 as a new Title 28 section.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-152 | The Commerce Legislation and Labor Aggravations Reformations (CLEAR)
laws.status_basis: The source is operative and contains a real replacement of 5 U.S.C. 101, plus repeal and transfer provisions aimed at earlier public laws, but later PL 31-226 expressly repeals PL 19-152 in its entirety. The act now survives only as history.

provisions.evidence: "5 U.S. Code 101 shall now state;"
provisions.notes: This replacement is later superseded by PL 31-226 in the chronology.
